issuesFound.title: missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. A descriptive page title is required for users to identify the purpose of the page and navigate between open tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design & Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: This button toggles the main navigation menu but lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 38: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: broken ARIA reference: element references id that does not exist
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-controls attribute references "resources-submenu", but the target <ul> on line 71 has no id attribute (or the id is missing from the inventory).
issuesFound.fix: Line 71: <ul id="resources-submenu" role="list" hidden>

issuesFound.title: non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Users navigating by links alone will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: Read more about our features (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image is missing an alt attribute. While the parent div has aria-hidden="true", the itself should have an empty alt attribute if it is decorative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148:

issuesFound.title: landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <section> elements on the page, but this one lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line 170: <section class="logo-bar" aria-label="Trusted brands">

issuesFound.title: table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: In a comparison table with multiple columns, the top-level <th> elements require scope="col" to correctly associate headers with data cells.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 488: <th scope="col">Starter</th>
Line 489: <th scope="col">Pro</th>
Line 490: <th scope="col">Enterprise</th>

issuesFound.title: missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" input lacks an autocomplete attribute, failing SC 1.3.5 for personal data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The "Work email" input has no associated <label> element or aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 751:

issuesFound.title: missing legend
issuesFound.explanation: The <fieldset> containing interest checkboxes is missing a <legend> to provide context for the group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 775: <fieldset><legend>What are you interested in?</legend>

fpTitles: missing legend

errorMessage: Ollama HTTP 429: {"StatusCode":429,"Status":"429 Too Many Requests","error":"timed out waiting for a concurrent request slot"}
Ollama HTTP 429: {"StatusCode":429,"Status":"429 Too Many Requests","error":"timed out waiting for a concurrent request slot"}

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides an outline-offset. Without a non-zero outline, box-shadow, or border, there is no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements that do not match :focus-visible. If a browser fails to support :focus-visible or incorrectly categorizes an interaction, the focus indicator is lost entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the focus indicator is set to outline: none. In high-contrast mode, box-shadows are often disabled, making a visible outline mandatory for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 327: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a replacement (like a box-shadow or border) within the same rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline. Keyboard users will not be able to visually track their position when navigating to the site home link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus indicator. This prevents keyboard users on mobile-width viewports from identifying the active element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the outline and only provide an outline-offset. Since the outline width is zero by default (due to the global reset), no indicator appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While it provides a box-shadow, box-shadows are often stripped in High Contrast Mode, leaving the element without an indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline for focus-visible states, which are specifically intended for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to verify they have reached the submit action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline, failing to provide a visible indicator for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the focus outline, which is a failure of SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus indicator, preventing keyboard users from knowing which category filter they are about to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle buttons remove the focus outline, creating a barrier for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1301: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table container is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable region is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1434: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus outline, making it difficult for keyboard users to navigate the list of questions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls remove the outline on focus. While a box-shadow is provided, it is insufficient for users in High Contrast Mode where shadows are removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1638: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, which is the primary indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1639: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 561: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: min-width: 24px; Line 575: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class has a min-height of 32px. While it meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px AAA recommendation and may be difficult to use without sufficient spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 488: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle is sized at 28px, which is significantly smaller than the recommended 44px target for critical navigation elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 606: min-height: 44px; Line 607: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 658: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links are sized at 28px height, which may lead to accidental activations on touch devices due to the small target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 682: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size for form inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px by 28px, which is a small target for a primary search action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 734: width: 44px; Line 735: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1283: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product CTA buttons are restricted to a 28px min-height, making them harder to tap on mobile devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1211: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class resets width/height to auto and overflow to visible. This fails to visually hide the content if the element contains text, and the lack of clipping means it may interfere with the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: width: 1px; Line 347: height: 1px; Line 350: overflow: hidden; Line 351: clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); clip-path: inset(50%); position: absolute;

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting word-spacing to -0.2em crowds text together, making it difficult for users with low vision or dyslexia to distinguish individual words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing (-0.05em) reduces the legibility of body text by causing characters to overlap or appear too close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly degrades readability for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but there is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes a transform transition. Users who are sensitive to motion may experience vestibular distress when buttons move on hover/active states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The arrow icon in feature card links translates horizontally on hover. This spatial movement lacks a reduced motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1000: .feature-card a::after { content: "→"; transition: none; display: inline-block; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion arrow rotates via a transition. While a media query exists on line 1588, it only targets the transition property, not the transform itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1588: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global reset removes underlines from all links. In body text (like .prose or paragraphs), this makes links indistinguishable from text for colorblind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.32:1
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary text color (--colour-text-secondary) used against the page background (--colour-bg-page) fails the minimum contrast requirement of 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 74: --colour-text-secondary: #656565;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #ffffff ratio 2.54:1
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text color (--colour-neutral-400) used in inputs against a white background fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio for informative text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 23: --colour-neutral-400: #6b7280;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.18:1
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker (.required-marker) uses --colour-error (#ee9999) against a white background, making the "required" signal unreadable for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 38: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle CSS classes and scroll locks but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ...` Line 1461: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ...`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator adds an error message and CSS classes but fails to set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3098: `} else { srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); errorEl.textContent = result.firstError; ...`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected or cleared, the code removes error classes but does not set `aria-invalid` to `false` or remove the attribute. This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid even after the user has fixed the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3092: `if (result.valid) { srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ...` Line 3145: `function clearField(field) { ... field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); ...`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger button. Screen reader users will not know the initial state of the collapsible menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: `_initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); _trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function in the product filter module uses a `filterAnimation` that sets the `hidden` attribute on filtered-out cards but does not consistently apply `aria-hidden="true"`. This can lead to "ghost" elements remaining in the accessibility tree in some browser/SR combinations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2010: `toHide.forEach(el => { el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); el.style.opacity = ''; });`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` handler for the pricing switch changes all prices on the page (monthly vs annual) but does not trigger a live region announcement to inform the user that the data has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: `function _onToggleClick() { const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year'; setPeriod(next); liveRegion.announce(next === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` created in the toast manager uses the character '×' as its text content. Without an `aria-label`, screen readers will announce this as "times" or "multiplication sign" instead of "Close" or "Dismiss".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1315: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': STRINGS.labels.dismissToast || 'Dismiss' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input (`_emailEl`) lacks an `autocomplete` attribute. Since this field collects a user's email address (personal data), it must have `autocomplete="email"` to satisfy WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3315: `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl); if (_emailEl) { _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a `click` listener to a `zoneEl` (which is a `div`) but does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key, only for `Enter`. Native buttons respond to both.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle (`_toggleEl`) acts as a switch to control the pricing cards but does not have an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the container of the prices it modifies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2195: `if (_toggleEl) { const gridId = srUtils.ensureId(qs('[data-product-grid]'), 'pricing-grid'); _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-controls', gridId); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates the pricing cards and toggle UI but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the period buttons. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state of the toggle control is not programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `function _syncToggleState(period) { if (!_periodBtns) return; _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive)); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); }); ...`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` adds an error message and CSS classes but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: `function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { if (result.valid) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... } else { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... } }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger button. Interactive elements that control visibility should have their state defined on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: `_initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ...`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `filterAnimation` function hides product cards using the `hidden` attribute but does not consistently apply `aria-hidden="true"` to ensure they are removed from the accessibility tree in all browsers during the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2015: `toHide.forEach(el => { el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... });`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the pricing comparison table is expanded or collapsed via `setComparisonTableExpanded`, there is no live region announcement or focus management to inform the user that a large table has appeared in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: `if (expanded) { table.removeAttribute('hidden'); liveRegion.announce('Comparison table expanded'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` created in the `toastManager` uses a '×' character as its text content but lacks a descriptive `aria-label`. Screen reader users will hear "times button" or "X button" instead of "Dismiss notification".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': STRINGS.labels.dismissToast || 'Dismiss notification' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a `click` listener to the `zoneEl` (a `div`) but does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key, only `Enter`. Native buttons respond to both.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3415: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for "full_name" and "email" are missing the `autocomplete` attribute. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) for common user data fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3745: `// (In HTML or via JS) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', cfg.selector.includes('email') ? 'email' : 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Missing programmatic focus on dialog open
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in `cookieConsent` creates a dialog but does not immediately move focus to the dialog or its first focusable element, forcing keyboard users to tab through the entire page to reach the banner.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3645: `_bannerEl.appendChild(actions); document.body.appendChild(_bannerEl); _bannerEl.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); _bannerEl.focus();`

fpTitles: Missing accessible name
Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
Missing autocomplete on personal data input
Missing programmatic focus on dialog open

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-height 20px, min-width 20px

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] height 20px, width 20px

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class sets properties that make content visible on screen (position: static, overflow: visible, clip: auto). This does not hide content visually and may cause screen reader users to hear redundant content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: Replace with a correct visually hidden technique, e.g., position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover transform
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transform animation on hover, but the existing reduced-motion media query only targets .pricing-card:hover, not .pricing-card--featured:hover. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 926: Extend the reduced-motion media query to include .pricing-card--featured:hover, e.g., add .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body word-spacing -0.2em

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #64748b / background #555555 ratio ~1.24:1

issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Use a lighter text color that meets contrast requirements, e.g., color: var(--colour-neutral-300); or adjust the background color.

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none, removing underlines. Links are distinguished only by color, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 183: Add a non-color visual differentiator, such as text-decoration: underline; or a border-bottom on links.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the controlled menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 2040: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the open/closed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 2160: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on FAQ accordion trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close functions expand/collapse FAQ answers but do not set aria-expanded on the question button. Screen reader users are not informed of the expanded state of each FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); Line 3070: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function switches between monthly/annual billing but does not set aria-pressed on the period toggle buttons. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Add setAttr(activePeriodButton, ARIA.PRESSED, true); and setAttr(inactivePeriodButtons, ARIA.PRESSED, false); inside _syncToggleState.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates visible product cards and count badges but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filtered results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic FAQ open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close functions expand/collapse answers but do not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when an answer becomes available or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: Add liveRegion.announce(`Expanded: ${item.question}`); Line 3070: Add liveRegion.announce(`Collapsed: ${item.question}`);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic pricing change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates all plan prices on the page but does not announce the billing period change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when prices switch between monthly and annual.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Add liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${period} billing`); after _updateSavingsMessage.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users are not informed of the invalid state via ARIA.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: In the else block, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); In the valid block, add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the hamburger button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after _trigger assignment.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when panels are hidden on page load. Screen reader users are not informed of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for FAQ accordion triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqRegistry.parse function does not set aria-expanded on FAQ question buttons based on the initial open state. Screen reader users are not informed of the initial expanded/collapsed state of each item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, isInitiallyOpen); after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden mobile nav drawer not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. Screen reader users may still perceive the hidden drawer in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040: Add setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); after removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden dropdown panels not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides dropdown panels but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the panel element. Screen reader users may still perceive hidden dropdown content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Add setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true); after setting the hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open without focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function reveals the navigation drawer but does not move keyboard focus into the drawer. Keyboard users are trapped outside the opened menu, breaking navigation flow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Add const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus(); after _lockScroll.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open without focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals dropdown panels but does not move keyboard focus to the first focusable item in the panel. Keyboard users cannot immediately navigate dropdown content without manual tabbing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: Add const firstItem = getFocusableChildren(inst.panel)[0]; if (firstItem) firstItem.focus(); after removing hidden.

fpTitles: Dynamically-hidden dropdown panels not updated in accessibility tree
Mobile nav open without focus management

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu toggle functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.open function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. In mobileNav.close, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (month, quarter, year) act as a button group but do not set aria-pressed to indicate the selected state. Screen reader users cannot tell which option is active.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive);` for each button with data-period.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the grid updates with new products and a count. This dynamic content change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after updating the grid, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied?.(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: Price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches billing periods, all plan card prices update dynamically. This significant content change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In the setPeriod function, after updating all plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices updated');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu is closed on page load, but the trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen reader users may not know the button controls an expandable menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init function, after obtaining _trigger, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus are closed on page load, but their trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen reader users may not know these buttons control expandable menus.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu.register function, after ensuring ARIA attributes, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden visually but not removed from the accessibility tree with aria-hidden. Screen reader users may still navigate into the hidden drawer.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.close function, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In the open function, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu panel is closed, it is hidden but not removed from the accessibility tree with aria-hidden. Screen reader users may still navigate into hidden panels.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel slide changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel changes slides automatically and via user interaction, but the new slide content is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: In the goTo function, after updating the active slide, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselSlideAnnounce?.(target + 1, _slides.length) || 'Slide ' + (target + 1) + ' of ' + _slides.length);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion search filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering FAQ items by search query, the count of matching items is announced, but the hiding/showing of items is not announced. Screen reader users may not know the list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: In the _filterByQuery function, after updating visibility, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqResultsCount?.replace('{n}', count).replace('{s}', count !== 1 ? 's' : '') || count + ' questions found');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (data-filter-value) act as toggles for the product grid but do not have aria-controls pointing to the grid they control. Screen reader users may not understand the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: In the productFilter.init function, after obtaining _gridEl, set an id on it if missing, then for each tab button add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle triggers missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons are missing aria-controls pointing to their answer panels. Screen reader users may not understand which panel each button controls.
issuesFound.fix: In the faqRegistry.parse function, ensure panelId is set and then add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (role="switch") does not have aria-controls pointing to the pricing cards it updates. Screen reader users may not understand the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: In the pricing.init function, obtain a reference to the pricing cards container (e.g., SELECTORS.pricingSection) and set its id. Then add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', containerId);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu toggle button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer it controls. Screen reader users may not understand the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav.init function, after obtaining _drawer, ensure it has an id, then add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel's play/pause button toggles auto-play but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot tell if auto-play is active.
issuesFound.fix: In the play function, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');`. In the pause function, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid (contact form)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form validation marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _validateAll function, for each invalid field, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Ensure valid fields have `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: newsletter form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validation marks the email field invalid but does not set aria-invalid. Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onSubmit function, when validation fails, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. On successful validation/submission, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard validates step fields but does not set aria-invalid on invalid inputs. Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _validateCurrentStep function, for each invalid field, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. On valid steps, ensure `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list visibility not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion list is shown/hidden without updating aria-hidden. When hidden, it may remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In siteSearch._open, add `setAttr(suggestionListRenderer._listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`. In _close, add `setAttr(suggestionListRenderer._listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner visibility not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner is shown/hidden without updating aria-hidden on the banner. When hidden, it may remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In _showBanner, add `setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`. In _hideBanner, add `setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: toast notifications visibility not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications are shown/hidden without updating aria-hidden. When hidden (dismissed), they may remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In the toastManager.show function, after adding the toast, ensure `toast.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`. In the dismiss function, add `toast.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing discount code input validation not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code input is validated but does not set aria-invalid on invalid entries. Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onApply function, after validation, if invalid add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. If valid, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone is a non-native interactive element (div) with click and drag-drop but no keyboard support for Enter/Space activation.
issuesFound.fix: In the bind function, ensure the zone has `role="button"` and add a keydown handler: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (add this inside the `open` function)

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close` function hides the panel using `animateHeight` and `setAttribute('hidden', '')`, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. This may leave the content accessible to some ATs while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1673: `item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` (add this inside the `close` function)

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion._filterByQuery` function updates the visibility of FAQ items based on search input, but it does not announce the result count or "no results" status to a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1844: `liveRegion.announce(count === 0 ? STRINGS.faqNoResults : STRINGS.faqResultsCount.replace('{n}', count));` (ensure this is called after DOM updates)

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.pause` function stops the carousel but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the play/pause button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1996: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and inserts an error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2368: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` (add this inside the `else` block of `_renderResult`)

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2364: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (add this inside the `if (result.valid)` block)

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1143: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1155: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` functions toggle the visibility of the `_overlay` element using CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute, but they do not manage `aria-hidden`. This can lead to inconsistent accessibility tree states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `_overlay.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`, Line 1158: `_overlay.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visible state of the primary navigation, but no announcement is sent to a live region. Users of assistive technology will not receive confirmation that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1141: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`, Line 1153: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function cycles the `_playing` state, but the `_playBtn` does not have its `aria-pressed` attribute updated to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1858: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class (`CSS.fieldInvalid`) and updates the error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2148: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `inlineValidator._renderResult` removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2142: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines a transition that includes `transform`, creating spatial movement, but there is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced‑motion) rule to disable it, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Add within the existing reduced‑motion block:
  Line 640: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` has `transition: transform var(--transition-slower);` causing movement, but no reduced‑motion override disables it.
issuesFound.fix:
Add:
  Line 560: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies a transform to `.hero-image img:hover`, creating motion without a reduced‑motion safeguard.
issuesFound.fix:
Add:
  Line 570: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state adds `transform: translateY(-1px);` to the primary CTA button, causing motion without a reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix:
Add:
  Line 540: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: [data‑billing‑toggle] element missing aria‑checked (or aria‑selected) for a switch control
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period switch is given role="switch" but never receives aria‑checked to indicate its on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: When the toggle changes, call `setAttr(toggleEl, 'aria-checked', isOn ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens, the trigger’s aria‑expanded state is never updated, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `'false'` when closing.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle (simple on/off switch) missing aria‑pressed or aria‑checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The switch toggles visual state but does not expose its binary state via aria‑pressed or aria‑checked, violating ARIA button patterns.
issuesFound.fix: Update the element with `setAttr(toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', isOn ? 'true' : 'false')` or use aria‑checked for a switch.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is invalid, the code adds an error message but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` for invalid fields and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button lacks initial aria‑expanded attribute on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle’s aria‑expanded state is never set to false initially, leaving its state ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: During init, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button missing aria‑controls linking to the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The button does not reference the controlled navigation drawer via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` once the drawer has an id.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility changes are not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer opens/closes, only CSS classes are toggled; aria‑hidden is never added/removed, so the element remains in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: When opening, `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden')`; when closing, `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel hide/show does not update aria‑hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via hidden attribute but aria‑hidden is not set, leaving it perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: productFilter updates product count badge without announcing the change via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The numeric badge changes are not announced, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Create a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> and update its text with the count each time the badge changes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Users receive no audible feedback that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening/closing, set `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users receive no indication that the navigation drawer has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened')` or `announce('Navigation menu closed')`.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu panel hide/show does not update aria‑hidden state

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open dropdown panels never have their aria-expanded state toggled, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance and _closeInstance, add:
    setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); // when opening
    setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); // when closing

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (data‑billing‑toggle) changes visual state but does not expose its expanded/collapsed state via aria-expanded, breaking accessibility for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In setPeriod, after updating the UI, add:
    setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, period !== 'month' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not reflect selection via aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons receive a visual “active” class but never have aria-pressed toggled, so assistive technologies cannot tell which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the script removes error styling but never removes the aria‑invalid attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria-hidden removal on open
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the panel’s hidden attribute is removed but aria‑hidden remains set, causing screen readers to still treat the panel as hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In open(item), after panel.removeAttribute('hidden'), also call:
    panel.removeAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: Live region for search status is not announced assertively for errors
issuesFound.explanation: The search status element uses aria-live="polite". Errors (e.g., network failures) should be announced with aria-live="assertive" to ensure immediate AT notification.
issuesFound.fix: When setting an error message, change the live region’s aria-live attribute to "assertive".

issuesFound.title: Sticky header does not set aria-hidden on hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides on scroll, it is visually hidden but still perceivable to AT because aria-hidden is not set.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button does not have an accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The button lacks an aria-label or visible text, making its purpose unclear to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an aria-label attribute, e.g.:
    setAttr(_btn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons lack aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The previous/next buttons control a listbox but do not reference it via aria-controls, so AT cannot associate the controls with the carousel content.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs missing associated label (due to FormGroup issue)

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs missing aria-invalid when invalid

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs aria-describedby reference non‑existent IDs
issuesFound.explanation: Each input’s aria-describedby points to an ID like “contact-name-error” that is never rendered, so error messages are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure ErrorMessage components are rendered with matching IDs (e.g., <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" …/>) or adjust aria-describedby to the correct ID.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually. This defeats the purpose of the utility and may clutter the visual interface for sighted users while intending to hide from them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 606: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class has a min-height of 32px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating buttons on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 727: .btn { min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform and transition for a 3D effect. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 1115 is empty and does not disable the transform, causing motion for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transform and transition to slide into view. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 686 is empty and does not disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 686: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card uses transform on hover. The reduced motion override at Line 1392 targets .pricing-card:hover but has lower specificity than .pricing-card--featured:hover, so the motion persists for featured cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1392: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia by crowding words together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none. The hover state (Line 1602) only changes color, which relies on color alone to indicate interactivity, failing SC 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1602: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have text-decoration: none. The hover state (Line 1610) only changes color, relying on color alone to indicate interactivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements that do not override this rule, blocking keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 339: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions `open` and `close` modify the visibility of the drawer and body classes but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1295: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1315: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` toggle the `hidden` attribute on the panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). Assistive technologies will not reflect the correct expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1500: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1515: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion `open` and `close` functions animate the panel and update CSS classes but fail to set `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded/collapsed state correctly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2180: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2210: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search module's `_open` and `_close` functions show or hide the suggestion listbox but do not update `aria-expanded` on the search input (`_inputEl`). This breaks the combobox pattern for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1830: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1845: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function in the pricing module toggles the `is-active` class on billing period buttons (`[data-period]`) but does not update `aria-pressed`. Users relying on assistive technology will not know which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` helper function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1230: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` helper function removes the error description but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute from the field. The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility toggle misses aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_update` function in `scrollToTop` toggles the `hidden` attribute on the button (`_btn`) based on scroll position but does not update `aria-hidden`. The button may remain in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1650: setAttr(_btn, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); (in the hide block)
  Line 1650: _btn.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); (in the show block)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav overlay visibility toggle misses aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the `hidden` attribute on the overlay element (`_overlay`) but do not update `aria-hidden`. The overlay may be perceivable to screen readers when it should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1295: setAttr(_overlay, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 1315: setAttr(_overlay, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel visibility toggle misses aria-hidden

Line 1500: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list visibility toggle misses aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `show` and `hide` functions in `suggestionListRenderer` toggle the `hidden` attribute on the listbox (`_listEl`) but do not update `aria-hidden`. The suggestion list may be exposed to the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

Line 1830: setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');
  Line 1845: setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the navigation state but do not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.

Line 1295: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in `dropdownMenu` toggle the dropdown visibility but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
