issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Dropdown menu links should be large enough for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { display: block; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Form inputs should be large enough for easy interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Buttons should be large enough for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 790: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Filter buttons should be large enough for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1048: `.filter-tabs button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background: none; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Toggle buttons should be large enough for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: `.pricing-toggle button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on product card buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-body .btn-secondary selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Product card buttons should be large enough for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: `.product-body .btn-secondary { margin-top: var(--space-2); align-self: flex-start; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target meets minimum but below recommended on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum WCAG 2.5.8 requirement but falls below the 44px recommended size. Footer links should be larger for better usability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. Social media links should be large enough for easy activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1254: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement animations when buttons are activated. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: `transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast);`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement when the link appears. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from this animation without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: `transition: none;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition property, and the hover state applies a 3D transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 939 is empty, providing no override for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 939-942: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision. Negative word-spacing can cause words to overlap or appear cramped.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making content difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 428: `.section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against background var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 956: `.logo-bar-label { text-align: center; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-widest); margin-bottom: var(--space-8); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f1f5f9), resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1. Users with low vision may struggle to read helper text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1181: `.input-hint { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing section header
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-section .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f1f5f9), resulting in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: `.pricing-section .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on filter label
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-label selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, failing WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1043: `.filter-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing toggle label
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle > span selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f1f5f9), resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1196: `.pricing-toggle > span { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); white-space: nowrap; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer brand paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.7:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1241: `.footer-brand > p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.5:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1242: `.footer-brand address { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); line-height: var(--line-height-loose); font-style: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against #555555, resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1247: `.footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against #555555, resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against #555555, resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1263: `.footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against #555555, resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1266: `.footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against #555555, resulting in poor contrast below 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1270: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators such as font-weight or border-bottom, links are indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users, violating WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 137: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to links within body text paragraphs specifically.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a selector inherits text-decoration: none and relies solely on colour change for hover states. Without underlines or other non-colour indicators, users cannot distinguish links from text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: Add `text-decoration: underline;` to the default state or `border-bottom: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);` to the hover state to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when panels are shown or hidden. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (the close function only sets it to "false"). This creates an inconsistent state where screen readers report collapsed state even when content is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in faqAccordion.open): Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the code updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markInvalid): Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  Line (in markValid): Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the function before the closing brace

issuesFound.title: SUPPORTS.dom property missing causing initialization failures
issuesFound.explanation: The SUPPORTS object is missing the 'dom' property, which is checked by createFocusTrap, skipLinkManager, focusHistory, and keyboardNavigationGuard. These accessibility modules fail to initialize because SUPPORTS.dom is undefined, breaking focus management and keyboard navigation features.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in SUPPORTS object): Add `dom: typeof document !== 'undefined',` after the last property and before the closing parenthesis

issuesFound.title: pricing switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch element (with role="switch") does not update aria-checked to reflect the selected state (month vs year). Screen readers cannot convey the on/off state of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in _syncToggleState): Insert `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` inside the `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack)` block after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the drawer element is only hidden via CSS class removal but does not receive aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may still be able to access the visually hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in mobileNav.close): Insert `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line (in mobileNav.open): Insert `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, prices update visually with animation, but screen reader users are not notified of the price changes via a live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in pricing.setPeriod): Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices updated to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);` after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but it never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether a dropdown menu is currently open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _openInstance. Line 1425: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions manage the expanded state of answer panels but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons. This leaves screen reader users unaware of which accordion items are currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2624: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the open function. Line 2652: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search module manages a combobox pattern where the suggestion listbox is shown or hidden, but the aria-expanded attribute on the input is never updated to reflect the visibility state of the popup. Screen reader users cannot tell when suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2176: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _open. Line 2185: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _close.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to screen readers. This affects the period buttons and the main toggle switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2495: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` for the active period button and `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` for inactive buttons. Line 2540: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', next === 'year');` in _onToggleClick.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1166: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1183: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` in the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization finds the trigger button and drawer but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element, leaving screen readers unaware of the collapsed initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1376: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after confirming _trigger and _drawer exist in the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown menus, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but it does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button before the dropdown is first opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1506: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting aria-controls in the register function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
productFilter filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown or hidden, the function updates the count badge but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);` after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched between monthly and annual, the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2495: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the prices.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element, meaning it remains visible to screen readers even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1322: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close function, and `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap or activates it in the open function. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile menu into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1294: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` and `_trap.activate();` in the open function before locking scroll.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Although the _closeInstance function accepts a returnFocus parameter, the function body never uses this parameter to return focus to the trigger button when closing via Escape key or outside click.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1425: Add `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();` at the end of the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in the dropdownMenu module add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the dropdown panel, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1840: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance function.
  Line 1850: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;` inside the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets the item state to open and animates the panel height, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the answer panel has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function initialises the panel with the hidden attribute and sets up aria-controls on the trigger, but it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger when the menu is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` in the register function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function locates the trigger button and drawer elements, but does not establish the programmatic relationship with aria-controls or set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` and `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the elements are assigned in the init function.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs not updating aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function in productFilter toggles CSS active classes to show the selected filter, but it does not update the aria-pressed attribute on the tab buttons to indicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside the forEach loop.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not setting aria-invalid on errors
issuesFound.explanation: When the inlineValidator detects a validation error, it calls srUtils.markInvalid which only updates aria-describedby, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in the else block (invalid case) of the _renderResult function, after the call to srUtils.markInvalid.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the inlineValidator removes error styling and describedby references, but it does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", potentially leaving stale invalid states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2660: Insert `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` in the if (result.valid) block of the _renderResult function, after the call to srUtils.markValid.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and count badges after filtering, but it does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the setPeriod function updates the displayed prices but does not announce the billing period change or the new prices to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after updating the plan cards in the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions toggle the navigation drawer but do not announce the state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1380: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);` in the open function after setting _open = true.
  Line 1400: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` in the close function after setting _open = false.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus via _openInstance and _closeInstance does not trigger a live region announcement, so screen reader users are not informed when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1840: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} menu opened`);` in _openInstance after setting inst.open = true.
  Line 1860: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} menu closed`);` in _closeInstance after setting inst.open = false.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: Although the mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping, the open() function never initialises or activates the focus trap, allowing keyboard focus to escape the modal drawer to background content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1380: Insert `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` in the open function.
  Line 1400: Insert `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module implements a combobox pattern but does not update aria-expanded on the input element when the suggestion list opens or closes, preventing screen readers from announcing the popup state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1950: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` in the _open function.
  Line 1970: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` in the _close function.

issuesFound.title: Site search missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting up the input attributes in the init function.

issuesFound.title: Scroll to top button missing accessible name guarantee
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop.init() function selects the button element but does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or text content), which is required for screen readers to identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: Insert `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_btn.textContent.trim()) _btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');` after finding the button element.

issuesFound.title: File upload validation errors not associated with input
issuesFound.explanation: When the fileUploadField rejects a file for size or type violations, it announces the error via liveRegion but does not associate the error message with the file input using aria-describedby or aria-errormessage.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3200: Insert `srUtils.addDescribedBy(binding.inputEl, binding.statusEl);` in the _addFile function when a validation error occurs, and ensure the status element has an id.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form status region missing role on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletterForm._showError function sets the status element's role to "alert", but the _showSuccess function does not explicitly set role="status", which may cause inconsistent screen reader announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2720: Insert `_statusEl.setAttribute('role', 'status');` in the _showSuccess function before setting the text content.

issuesFound.title: Contact form error summary missing aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm._renderErrorSummary function creates an error summary container with role="alert" but does not ensure it has aria-live="assertive" for immediate announcement, and the focus move may not be sufficient for all screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: Insert `_errorSummaryEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'assertive');` and `_errorSummaryEl.setAttribute('aria-atomic', 'true');` when creating or updating the error summary element.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing aria-valuetext updates
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard._updateProgress function sets aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the progress bar, but the aria-valuetext format could be clearer for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: Change the aria-valuetext to `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', `Step ${stepIndex + 1} of ${_steps.length}: ${entering.title}`);` to include the step title for better context.

issuesFound.fix: Line ~1170: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: TextInput component not exposing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: When the invalid prop is true, the input element does not receive aria-invalid="true", so screen reader users are not informed of the validation error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~754: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component not exposing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: When the invalid prop is true, the textarea element does not receive aria-invalid="true".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~766: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons do not indicate which option is selected, failing to communicate the pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1081: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>
  Line ~1088: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div used as interactive element without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop overlay has an onClick handler but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~783: <div role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close navigation menu" className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div used as interactive element without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard support.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1386: <div role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close dialog" className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page navigation link is visually indicated but lacks aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~749: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~802: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state, it does not communicate this busy state to assistive technology, which is important for users who cannot see the visual spinner.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~580: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button spinner icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span is exposed to assistive technology despite being accompanied by visually hidden text, causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~591: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify this major page region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~835: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section lacks an accessible name via aria-label or aria-labelledby, even though it contains a heading that could label it.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~871: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element without aria-labelledby, despite having a heading that could provide its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1062: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a nav element without an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~624: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The main navigation lacks an aria-label, which is needed when multiple nav elements exist on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~738: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated integration list used for visual marquee scrolling is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~912: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon indicating submenu state is exposed to assistive technology as text content, causing redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~716: <span className="nav-link__chevron" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star symbols are exposed to assistive technology even though the rating value is already conveyed by the parent element's aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~643: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not marked as hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible carousel slides are not marked with aria-hidden="true", so screen reader users may encounter content that is not visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1130: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has incorrect aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay element instead of the dialog element, which may cause assistive technology to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1386: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dialog element lacks aria-modal="true", which is necessary to indicate that the content beneath the dialog is inert while the dialog is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1388: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual required field marker (asterisk) is exposed to assistive technology, which may announce "star" instead of the intended meaning.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~696: *
